Test Description: Verify default reset values and masked write-read behavior across the GPIO GP0 per-pad control registers, group interrupt registers, I/O control group registers, and data in/out group registers that are enumerated by this test.
Remarks: Certain registers are intentionally skipped for reset or write checks per skip lists; entries with non-readable or non-writable masks are also skipped. Note from the code: when reading default values the DIN value becomes 1 automatically if no force is applied; forcing zero to DIN causes the bit-level select to become high, leading to an expected-value mismatch in that condition.
Test Steps / Procedure: At startup, the test iterates over a fixed list of forty-nine GPIO-related registers. For each register in this list, if the entry is flagged to skip reset verification or is marked as not readable, it is skipped; otherwise the current value is read, the least significant bit is masked off, and the result is compared against the expected reset value, with a default-mismatch counter incremented for any difference. After completing the default checks, the test selects a sequence of six data patterns and, for each pattern, walks the same list and writes the pattern masked by each register’s write mask to every entry that is not flagged to skip and that is writable. For each written pattern, the test again walks the list and, for each entry that is not skipped and that is both writable and readable, reads back the value and masks it by the corresponding read mask. For every such read, the expected value is computed by combining the written bits that are permitted by both the write and read masks with the preserved default bits for any write-protected fields; the observed value is then compared to this expectation, and a write-read-mismatch counter is incremented upon any discrepancy. When all patterns and comparisons complete, the test concludes with a pass if both failure counters are zero; otherwise it reports failure.

Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass if no default-value mismatches are detected and no write-read mismatches are detected across all targeted registers and all data patterns; the test terminates with a success status when both failure counters are zero, and with a failure status otherwise.
Gap Analysis: No interrupt or timing behavior is exercised; soft-reset sequence is present but compiled out; no negative testing of illegal accesses; no explicit error handling for lower-level read/write failures.

Test Description: Validate that a falling-edge on each GPIO pad from 8 through 39 generates a group interrupt, is serviced correctly, and is fully cleared at both the GPIO and system-interrupt levels.
Remarks: Build-time selection of the GPIO instance via GPIO0 or GPIO1 determines which interrupt line is enabled and which system-interrupt bit is cleared. Pad state stimulus is produced by writes to address 0xA0243ffc. Per-pin configuration enables input and edge detection before testing. The loop index is used by the interrupt handler to identify the active pin.
Test Steps / Procedure: At start, the platform interrupt for the selected GPIO instance is enabled and the corresponding system-level interrupt output is unmasked. A known drive pattern is applied so that all pads begin in a high state. The test configures each of the thirty-two targeted pads in sequence so that input sensing and edge detection are enabled, pausing briefly after configuration. Next, for each pad index in order, the test clears any raw pending interrupt state for just that pad, unmasks the group interrupt output only for that pad, and waits briefly. It then arms an internal flag to indicate that an interrupt is expected, applies a high state to all pads, waits for a short settling time, and then drives only the current pad low to produce a single falling edge. The main thread then waits in a bounded loop for the interrupt handler to clear the internal flag; if the flag does not clear before the timeout expires, an error is recorded for that pad. When an interrupt occurs, the handler clears the internal flag, restores the pad drive to a known high state, reads the per-pad status for the current index and records an error if the input-level bit indicates an unexpected high level, confirms that the group-status register contains the bit corresponding to the active pad and records an error if it does not, re-arms the per-pad control and clears the raw per-pad interrupt status, verifies that the group-status register has returned to zero, and finally clears the system-level raw interrupt and the platform interrupt controller’s pending state for the selected GPIO instance. After all pads have been exercised or a timeout has been encountered, the test reports the cumulative error count as its result.

Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass if, for each pad: the main thread does not hit the timeout while waiting for service; the handler observes an input-level consistent with a falling edge, the group-status bit corresponding to the pad is set during service and returns to zero after clearing, the per-pad raw status is cleared, and the system-level and platform interrupt pending states are cleared; the overall result is success when the cumulative error count is zero.
Gap Analysis: The handler depends on the loop index from the main thread to identify the pad, which can be fragile; there is no negative testing (e.g., spurious edges), no debounce/noise tolerance checks, and no validation of simultaneous multiple-pad events.

Test Description: Validate that a rising-edge on each GPIO pad from 8 through 39 generates a group interrupt, is serviced by masking, raw-status clearing, and system-level clearing, and that the group interrupt can be re-enabled for subsequent events.
Remarks: Build-time selection of the GPIO instance via GPIO0 or GPIO1 determines which interrupt line is used. Pad stimulus is generated by writes to address 0xA0243ffc. The test configures all targeted pads for rising-edge detection and sets the I/O control groups to input mode before enabling the group interrupt mask.
Test Steps / Procedure: The platform interrupt for the selected GPIO instance is enabled and the corresponding system-level interrupt output is unmasked. Each of the thirty-two targeted pads is configured for rising-edge detection. After a brief delay, the I/O control group registers are programmed so that the targeted pads are treated as inputs, and the test waits again. The group interrupt enable mask is then set to unmask all targeted pads. For each pad index in order, the test first drives the pad group low and waits briefly, arms an internal flag indicating that an interrupt is expected, and then drives the pad group high to produce a single rising edge. The main thread then waits in a bounded loop for the interrupt handler to clear the internal flag; if the flag does not clear before the timeout expires, an error is recorded and the loop exits early. After each serviced event, the drive is returned low and the test waits briefly before proceeding to the next index. In the interrupt handler, the internal flag is cleared, the current group interrupt status is read and the group output is temporarily masked, success is recorded if any pad bit is present and an error is recorded otherwise, the raw per-pad interrupt status for all targeted pads is cleared, a short delay is observed, the group status is read again and an error is recorded if it is nonzero, the system-level raw interrupt for the selected instance is cleared and verified to be deasserted, the group interrupt mask is restored to allow subsequent events, and the platform interrupt pending state is cleared. The test ends by reporting success when no errors have been accumulated.

Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass if, for each pad: the main thread does not hit the timeout while waiting for service; the handler observes nonzero group status during service and zero after raw-status clearing, the system-level raw status is cleared successfully, the group interrupt mask is restored, and the overall error count remains zero; the test reports success when the cumulative error count is zero.
